zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Pierce於1564年6月18日出生在英國素福爾可福雷斯頓時，她的父親Robert Pearse25歲，母親Mrs. Robert Pearse21歲。她於1588年5月15日與George Everard在英國英格蘭素福爾可霍爾布魯克結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Pierce于1564年6月18日出生在英国素福爾可福雷斯頓时，她的父亲Robert Pearse25岁，母亲Mrs. Robert Pearse21岁。她于1588年5月15日与George Everard在英国英格兰素福爾可霍爾布魯克结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Pierce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, and Irish: from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Piers, Peres, Peris, an Old French nominative of Pierre or Per, the French form of Peter . Pierce is especially frequent in northwestern Wales and Lancashire, while Pearce is the more common spelling in the rest of England. In Ireland Pierce and Pearse represent a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Piarais ‘son of Piaras’, a Gaelicized form of Piers.
Americanized form of some similar-sounding Jewish surname.
History: Franklin Pierce (1804–69), 14th president of the US, was born in Hillsborough, NH, on the New England frontier. His English ancestor Thomas Pierce emigrated to Charlestown, MA, in 1633/34.

variants.name: Pyers
meanings: English (North Yorkshire) and Scottish (Ayrshire): variant of   Pierce  . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Peirce
meanings: History: The name Peirce first appears in colonial American records in 1623with William Peirce, an English shipmaster who compiled the firstalmanac in English America.

variants.name: Piers
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Perrell
meanings: from a short form of the Middle English personal name Peres, Peris (from Old French Pierre; see Pierce ) + the diminutive suffix -el.
habitational name from a lost or unidentified place.

variants.name: Pearce
meanings: Welsh, English, and Irish: variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

variants.name: Parkin
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Perkin, Parkin, a pet form of Peres ‘Peter’ (see Pierce ) with the diminutive suffix -kin. (The change from -er- to -ar- was a characteristic phonetic development in Middle English.)

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moira
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Máire , now used throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Pierce was born on 18 June 1564, in Freston, Suffolk, England, her father, Robert Pearse, was 25 and her mother, Mrs. Robert Pearse, was 21. She married George Everard on 15 May 1588, in Holbrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Pierce est née le 18 juin 1564, à Freston, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert Pearse, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Robert Pearse, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé George Everard le 15 mai 1588, à Holbrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Pierce am 18. Juni 1564 in Freston, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Pearse 25 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Robert Pearse 21. Sie hat am 15. Mai 1588 in Holbrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom George Everard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Pierce è nata il il 18 giugno 1564 nel Freston, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Robert Pearse, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Robert Pearse, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato George Everard il 15 maggio 1588, nel Holbrook, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Pierceが1564年6月18日にイギリススフォルクフレストンで生まれた時，父Robert Pearseは25歳，母Mrs. Robert Pearseは21歳であった。1588年5月15日にイギリスイギリススフォルクホールブルークでGeorge Everardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Pierce(이)가 1564년 6월 18일 영국 수폴크 프레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Pearse(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Robert Pearse(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1588년 5월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 홀브룩에서 George Everard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Pierce nasceu em 18 de junho de 1564, em Freston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Pearse, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Robert Pearse, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com George Everard em 15 de maio de 1588, em Holbrook, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Pierce родилась 18 июня 1564 г. в местности фрестон, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Pearse было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Robert Pearse, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Everard 15 мая 1588 г. в местности холбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Pierce nació el 18 de junio de 1564, en Freston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Pearse, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Robert Pearse, tenía 21 años. Se casó con George Everard el 15 de mayo de 1588, en Holbrook, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas.
